title: matricaria chamomilla f. suaveolens
intro: Matricaria chamomilla f. suaveolens, also known as piney chamomile, is a variety of the matricaria chamomilla plant, belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae), characterized by its distinctive piney or apple-like scent and sweet, apple-like flavor. The medicinal properties of Matricaria chamomilla f. suaveolens include its anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antiseptic properties, which contribute to its effectiveness in soothing digestive issues, reducing anxiety and stress, and promoting relaxation. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to alleviate menstrual cramps, insomnia, and restlessness, as well as its use as a natural remedy for soothing digestive problems, such as irritable bowel syndrome and stomach ulcers. Matricaria chamomilla f. suaveolens is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, tinctures, and essential oils, which can be taken internally or applied topically to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and alleviate various health issues.
